title: GCI Salesforce Integration Guide
markdown:
# GCI Salesforce + ChatGPT Custom GPT Action Integration Guide

**Date:** 2026-03-02
**Prepared by:** Sagemind AI (Justin Headley)
**Due Diligence:** All claims categorized per `docs/standards/due-diligence.md`
**For:** Consultant walkthrough — step-by-step implementation reference

---

## Verification Key

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **V** | **Verified** — Confirmed against official primary documentation (Salesforce Help, OpenAI Docs, or OpenAI Cookbook) |
| **SS** | **Strongly Supported** — Multiple reliable sources agree; not directly testable without credentials |
| **U** | **Unverified** — Flagged; verify during implementation |

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Prerequisites and Context](#1-prerequisites-and-context)
2. [Salesforce Connected App Setup](#2-salesforce-connected-app-setup)
3. [ChatGPT Action Configuration](#3-chatgpt-action-configuration)
4. [OpenAPI Schema](#4-openapi-schema)
5. [GPT System Instructions](#5-gpt-system-instructions)
6. [Testing Plan](#6-testing-plan)
7. [Security Considerations](#7-security-considerations)
8. [Common Pitfalls](#8-common-pitfalls)
9. [GCI-Specific Considerations](#9-gci-specific-considerations)
10. [Critical Timing Issue: Spring '26 Changes](#10-critical-timing-issue-spring-26-changes)
11. [Sources](#11-sources)

---

## 1. Prerequisites and Context

### What GCI Already Has
- Salesforce CRM (with Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities, likely custom objects) **[SS]**
- ChatGPT Enterprise with a Custom GPT called "GCI Data Scout" **[V — we built it]**
- GCI Data Scout already has working Egnyte and ACC (Autodesk) integrations via OAuth Actions **[V — we built and tested these]**

### What We Are Adding
A third Action in the same GPT that connects to Salesforce via OAuth, allowing GCI employees to query Salesforce data (contacts, accounts, opportunities, custom objects) using natural language.

### Pattern
This follows the exact same pattern as the existing ACC integration: Connected App (Salesforce's equivalent of APS App) provides OAuth credentials, we write an OpenAPI schema, add it as an Action in ChatGPT, and update the system instructions for multi-source routing.

### Official Reference
OpenAI maintains an official Salesforce GPT Action example in their cookbook. We base our approach on this but customize it significantly for GCI's CRM use case (the cookbook example targets Service Cloud Cases). **[V]**

**Source:** [OpenAI Cookbook: GPT Actions - Salesforce](https://developers.openai.com/cookbook/examples/chatgpt/gpt_actions_library/gpt_action_salesforce/) | [GitHub notebook](https://github.com/openai/openai-cookbook/blob/main/examples/chatgpt/gpt_actions_library/gpt_action_salesforce.ipynb)

---

## 2. Salesforce Connected App Setup

This is performed by GCI's Salesforce administrator. Walk them through each step.

### 2.1 Navigate to App Manager

1. Log into Salesforce as an admin
2. Click the **gear icon** (top right) > **Setup** **[V]**
3. In the Quick Find search box (left sidebar), type **"App Manager"** **[V]**
4. Click **App Manager** under Apps **[V]**
5. Click **"New Connected App"** button (top right) **[V]**

**Source:** [Salesforce Help: Create a Connected App for API Integration](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.connected_app_create_api_integration.htm&language=en_US&type=5)

### 2.2 Basic Information Section

| Field | Value | Notes |
|-------|-------|-------|
| Connected App Name | `ChatGPT GCI Data Scout` | Any descriptive name **[V]** |
| API Name | Auto-populates from name | Leave as-is **[V]** |
| Contact Email | GCI admin's email | Required field **[V]** |
| Description | (Optional) | e.g., "OAuth integration for ChatGPT Custom GPT" |

### 2.3 API (Enable OAuth Settings) Section

Check the box: **"Enable OAuth Settings"** **[V]**

This reveals the OAuth configuration fields:

#### Callback URL (Placeholder)

Enter a placeholder callback URL — we will replace this later with the real one from ChatGPT:

```
https://chat.openai.com/aip/placeholder/oauth/callback
```

**Why a placeholder:** ChatGPT generates the actual callback URL only after you save the Action configuration. You need to create the Connected App first, get the credentials, configure the Action in ChatGPT, get the real callback URL, then come back and update this field. **[V]**

The real callback URL format will be:
```
https://chat.openai.com/aip/{g-GPT-ID}/oauth/callback
```
or
```
https://chatgpt.com/aip/{g-GPT-ID}/oauth/callback
```

**Both URLs must be registered** as valid callbacks in the Connected App. **[V]**

**Source:** [OpenAI: GPT Action Authentication](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/actions/authentication/)

#### Selected OAuth Scopes

Move the following scopes from "Available OAuth Scopes" to "Selected OAuth Scopes":

| Scope | Purpose | Recommendation |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| **`full`** | Full access to all data accessible by the logged-in user | Simplest option — recommended for initial setup **[V]** |
| **`api`** | Access to REST API, Bulk API | More restrictive alternative to `full` **[V]** |
| **`refresh_token` / `offline_access`** | Allows refresh token to be returned for persistent sessions | **Required if using `api` instead of `full`** **[V]** |

**Recommendation for GCI:** Start with **`full`** scope. This is what the OpenAI Cookbook recommends and matches the ACC integration pattern. You can tighten to `api` + `refresh_token` later once everything is confirmed working. **[V]**

**Important:** Even if you select `full`, you must **also** explicitly select `refresh_token` / `offline_access` if you want refresh tokens. The `full` scope does not automatically include refresh token capability. **[SS]**

**Source:** [Salesforce: OAuth Scope Parameter Values](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/mobile-sdk/guide/oauth-scope-parameter-values.html)

#### OAuth Flow Checkboxes

| Checkbox | Setting | Why |
|----------|---------|-----|
| **Enable Client Credentials Flow** | **Check** | Enables server-to-server authentication. The OpenAI cookbook requires this. **[V]** |
| **Enable Authorization Code and Credentials Flow** | **Check** | This is the primary OAuth flow ChatGPT uses (Authorization Code Grant). **[V]** |
| **Enable Token Exchange Flow** | **Check** | Required per OpenAI cookbook. Enables token exchange. **[V]** |
| **Require Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) Extension** | **UNCHECK (critical)** | ChatGPT Actions do NOT support PKCE. If this is checked, authentication will fail silently. **[V]** |

**Source:** [OpenAI Cookbook: Salesforce GPT Action](https://github.com/openai/openai-cookbook/blob/main/examples/chatgpt/gpt_actions_library/gpt_action_salesforce.ipynb)

#### Other Settings in This Section

| Setting | Value | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Require Secret for Web Server Flow | Leave checked (default) | Standard security **[SS]** |
| Require Secret for Refresh Token Flow | Leave checked (default) | Standard security **[SS]** |
| Enable Token Exchange Flow | Check | Same as above checkbox **[V]** |

### 2.4 Save the Connected App

Click **Save**. You will see a message:

> "Allow from 2-10 minutes for your changes to take effect on the server before using the connected app."

**This propagation delay is real.** Do not attempt to test OAuth until at least 10 minutes after saving. Some sources report delays of up to 10 minutes, especially for new Connected Apps. **[SS]**

**Source:** [Salesforce Developers: Create a Connected App](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/mobile-sdk/guide/connected-apps-howto.html), [Aquiva Labs: External Client Apps Deep Dive](https://aquivalabs.com/blog/external-client-apps-the-next-generation-of-app-interoperability/)

### 2.5 Retrieve Consumer Key and Consumer Secret

1. After saving, click **"Manage Consumer Details"** button (under the Consumer Key and Secret section) **[V]**
2. Salesforce will send a **verification code to the admin's email** — enter it **[V]**
3. Copy the **Consumer Key** (this becomes the ChatGPT Client ID) **[V]**
4. Copy the **Consumer Secret** (this becomes the ChatGPT Client Secret) **[V]**

**Store these securely.** Share with the consultant via a secure channel (not email).

### 2.6 Configure OAuth Policies

After saving, you need to configure access policies:

1. Return to the Connected App page in App Manager **[V]**
2. Click the dropdown arrow next to the app name > **Manage** **[V]**
3. Click **"Edit Policies"** **[V]**
4. Under **OAuth Policies**:

| Setting | Recommended Value | Notes |
|---------|-------------------|-------|
| Permitted Users | **All users may self-authorize** | Simplest for initial setup. Users authenticate themselves. **[V]** |
| IP Relaxation | **Relax IP restrictions** | ChatGPT's servers will have varying IPs; you cannot whitelist them. **[SS]** |
| Refresh Token Policy | **Refresh token is valid until revoked** (default) | Keeps the ChatGPT session alive indefinitely. **[V]** |

5. Also check **"Enable Token Exchange Flow"** if present on this page **[V]**
6. Click **Save** **[V]**

**Alternative for Permitted Users:** If GCI's security policy requires tighter control, use **"Admin approved users are pre-authorized"** and then assign specific Profiles or Permission Sets. However, this adds setup complexity. **[V]**

**Source:** [Salesforce Help: Manage OAuth Access Policies](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.connected_app_manage_oauth.htm&language=en_US&type=5)

### 2.7 Install the Connected App (CRITICAL — Post-September 2025)

Since September 2025, Salesforce requires Connected Apps to be explicitly **installed** in the org, not just created. Without this step, users may be blocked from authenticating. **[V]**

1. Go to **Setup** > search for **"Connected Apps OAuth Usage"** **[V]**
2. Find the **ChatGPT GCI Data Scout** app in the list **[V]**
3. Click **Install** **[V]**

If the app was created in the same org (which it was), it should be automatically "installed." But verify this. If it shows as "Uninstalled," click Install. **[SS]**

**Source:** [Salesforce Admins Blog: Connected App Usage Restriction Changes](https://admin.salesforce.com/blog/2025/get-ready-for-changes-to-connected-app-usage-restrictions)

### 2.8 Identify the Instance URL

The admin needs to provide the Salesforce **My Domain** URL. Find it:

1. **Setup** > search for **"My Domain"** **[V]**
2. The URL will look like: `https://gci.my.salesforce.com` or `https://gci.lightning.force.com` **[V]**
3. You need the **My Domain** format: `{instance}.my.salesforce.com` **[V]**

**What to collect from this step:** The `{instance}` part (e.g., `gci`, `gci-dev`, etc.)

---

## 3. ChatGPT Action Configuration

### 3.1 Open the GPT Editor

1. Go to ChatGPT (chat.openai.com or chatgpt.com)
2. Navigate to **My GPTs** > find **"GCI Data Scout"** **[V — existing GPT]**
3. Click **Edit** (or the pencil icon) **[V]**
4. Click on the **Configure** tab at the top **[V]**
5. Scroll down to the **Actions** section **[V]**

### 3.2 Add a New Action

1. Click **"Create new action"** **[V]**
2. This opens the Action editor with three sections: Authentication, Schema, and Privacy Policy **[V]**

**Note:** The existing Egnyte and ACC actions remain intact. Each action has its own schema and authentication. A single GPT can have multiple actions with different OAuth configurations. **[SS]**

### 3.3 Configure Authentication

Click **"Authentication"** and select **"OAuth"** **[V]**

Fill in the following fields:

| Field | Value | Notes |
|-------|-------|-------|
| **Client ID** | `{Consumer Key from Salesforce}` | From step 2.5 **[V]** |
| **Client Secret** | `{Consumer Secret from Salesforce}` | From step 2.5 **[V]** |
| **Authorization URL** | `https://{instance}.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize` | Replace `{instance}` with GCI's My Domain **[V]** |
| **Token URL** | `https://{instance}.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token` | Same instance name **[V]** |
| **Scope** | `full` | Or `api refresh_token` if using restrictive scopes **[V]** |
| **Token Exchange Method** | **Default (POST)** | Leave as default **[V]** |

**CRITICAL — Domain Matching:** The Authorization URL, Token URL, and the API server URL in your OpenAPI schema **must all share the same root domain** (`*.my.salesforce.com`). Using `login.salesforce.com` for auth URLs while having `{instance}.my.salesforce.com` as the server URL can cause validation errors. Use the My Domain URL for all three. **[V]**

**Why not `login.salesforce.com`?** While Salesforce supports `login.salesforce.com` as a generic OAuth endpoint, ChatGPT Actions enforce a rule that the auth URLs and API hostname must share a root domain. Using `{instance}.my.salesforce.com` for everything satisfies this requirement. **[SS]**

**Source:** [OpenAI Community: Auth URL domain matching](https://community.openai.com/t/gpt-actions-error-auth-url-token-url-and-api-hostname-must-share-a-root-domain/491072), [OpenAI Cookbook: Salesforce](https://developers.openai.com/cookbook/examples/chatgpt/gpt_actions_library/gpt_action_salesforce/)

### 3.4 Paste the OpenAPI Schema

In the **Schema** section, paste the OpenAPI YAML schema (see Section 4 below). **[V]**

### 3.5 Save and Get the Callback URL

1. Click **Save** on the Action **[V]**
2. After saving, ChatGPT displays the **Callback URL** at the top of the action configuration **[V]**
3. It will be in the format: `https://chat.openai.com/aip/g-{GPT_ID}/oauth/callback` **[V]**
4. **Copy this URL** — you need it for the next step **[V]**

### 3.6 Update the Callback URL in Salesforce

Go back to Salesforce and update the Connected App's callback URL:

1. **Setup** > **App Manager** **[V]**
2. Find the Connected App > click the dropdown > **Edit** **[V]**
3. In the OAuth section, **replace the placeholder callback URL** with the real one from ChatGPT **[V]**
4. **Add both variants** of the callback URL (one per line): **[V]**
   ```
   https://chat.openai.com/aip/g-{GPT_ID}/oauth/callback
   https://chatgpt.com/aip/g-{GPT_ID}/oauth/callback
   ```
5. Click **Save** **[V]**
6. **Wait 2-10 minutes** for the change to propagate **[SS]**

---

## 4. OpenAPI Schema

### 4.1 Schema Design Philosophy

Unlike the ACC integration (which has many specific endpoints for RFIs, Issues, Submittals, etc.), the Salesforce integration centers on **one powerful generic endpoint**: the SOQL query endpoint. This single endpoint can query any Salesforce object with any combination of fields, filters, and sorting. **[V]**

We augment this with:
- **SOSL search endpoint** — for cross-object full-text search **[V]**
- **Describe endpoint** — for discovering fields on unknown objects **[V]**
- **Pagination endpoint** — for retrieving additional result batches **[V]**

### 4.2 The Schema

A complete OpenAPI schema has already been prepared and is available at:

**`/Users/justin/Documents/Projects/sagemind/project_sandbox/gci/salesforce_openapi_schema.yaml`**

This schema defines four endpoints:

| Endpoint | operationId | Purpose |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `GET /services/data/v66.0/query` | `executeSoqlQuery` | Execute a SOQL query against any object **[V]** |
| `GET /services/data/v66.0/query/{nextRecordsIdentifier}` | `getNextQueryResults` | Retrieve next batch when results exceed 2000 records **[V]** |
| `GET /services/data/v66.0/search` | `executeSoslSearch` | Full-text search across multiple objects simultaneously **[V]** |
| `GET /services/data/v66.0/sobjects/{objectName}/describe` | `describeObject` | Get field metadata for any Salesforce object **[V]** |

**API Version:** v66.0 (Spring '26 — current as of March 2026) **[V]**

**Source:** [Salesforce REST API Developer Guide v66.0](https://resources.docs.salesforce.com/latest/latest/en-us/sfdc/pdf/api_rest.pdf)

### 4.3 Instance URL Handling

The schema uses a placeholder server URL:
```yaml
servers:
  - url: https://INSTANCE_NAME.my.salesforce.com
```

**This must be replaced** with GCI's actual My Domain before pasting into ChatGPT. For example:
```yaml
servers:
  - url: https://gci.my.salesforce.com
```

The instance URL is **hardcoded** in the schema because ChatGPT Actions require a fixed server URL. Salesforce's OAuth token response does include a dynamic `instance_url` field, but ChatGPT Actions cannot dynamically change the server URL based on token responses. **[SS]**

### 4.4 SOQL Query Parameter

The SOQL query is passed as a `q` query parameter and must be URL-encoded. The GPT system instructions teach the model how to construct these queries. Examples:

```
# Find contacts named Smith
GET /services/data/v66.0/query?q=SELECT+Id,+FirstName,+LastName,+Email,+Phone+FROM+Contact+WHERE+LastName+%3D+%27Smith%27+LIMIT+25

# Get open opportunities over $100K
GET /services/data/v66.0/query?q=SELECT+Id,+Name,+StageName,+Amount,+CloseDate,+Account.Name+FROM+Opportunity+WHERE+Amount+>+100000+AND+IsClosed+%3D+false+LIMIT+50
```

**Important:** Always include a `LIMIT` clause. Without one, Salesforce returns up to 2000 records per batch, which can easily exceed ChatGPT's 100K character response limit. **[V]**

### 4.5 SOQL vs SOSL — When to Use Which

| Feature | SOQL (`/query`) | SOSL (`/search`) |
|---------|-----------------|------------------|
| Queries | Single object with precise filters | Multiple objects with keyword search |
| Use when | "Show me contacts where last name is Smith" | "Find everything related to Acme" |
| Filter style | SQL-like WHERE clauses | Full-text keyword search |
| Returns | Exact matches on specified fields | Fuzzy matches across all indexed fields |
| Performance | Fast for targeted queries | Fast for broad searches |
| Pagination | Yes (nextRecordsUrl) | Limited (max 2000 results) |

**[V]** — Based on Salesforce REST API documentation

### 4.6 Describe Endpoint — Why It Matters

The `describeObject` endpoint is critical for a generic CRM query tool. When the GPT encounters an unknown object name (especially custom objects ending in `__c`), it can call `describe` to discover available fields before constructing a query. This prevents SOQL errors from referencing nonexistent fields. **[V]**

**Warning:** Describe responses can be very large (dozens of KB for objects with many fields). The GPT should focus on the `fields` array and specifically the `name`, `label`, `type`, and `nillable` properties. **[SS]**

---

## 5. GPT System Instructions

### 5.1 Updated Multi-Source Routing

The existing GCI Data Scout system instructions route between Egnyte and ACC. We need to add Salesforce as a third source. The routing logic becomes:

```
- Project files, specs, drawings, photos, PDFs --> Egnyte search
- RFIs --> ACC searchRfis
- Issues, punchlist items --> ACC listIssues
- Submittals --> ACC searchSubmittals
- Change orders, cost, budgets --> ACC cost endpoints
- Contacts, people, phone numbers, emails --> Salesforce SOQL (Contact)
- Companies, accounts, clients, subcontractors --> Salesforce SOQL (Account)
- Deals, bids, proposals, pipeline, revenue --> Salesforce SOQL (Opportunity)
- CRM data, custom objects --> Salesforce SOQL or SOSL
- "Find everything about [name/company]" --> Salesforce SOSL cross-object search
```

### 5.2 Salesforce-Specific Instructions to Add

Add the following section to the system instructions:

```
## Salesforce CRM Search

Use Salesforce for people, companies, deals, and CRM records. You have four Salesforce tools:

### executeSoqlQuery — Structured queries
Construct SOQL queries for precise data retrieval. Always:
- Include a LIMIT clause (default LIMIT 25, max LIMIT 50) to keep responses under 100K chars
- URL-encode the query string
- Use relationship notation for related objects (e.g., Account.Name on Contact)

Common SOQL patterns:
- Contacts: SELECT Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Account.Name FROM Contact WHERE [condition] LIMIT 25
- Accounts: SELECT Id, Name, Industry, BillingCity, BillingState, Phone, Website FROM Account WHERE [condition] LIMIT 25
- Opportunities: SELECT Id, Name, StageName, Amount, CloseDate, Account.Name, Owner.Name FROM Opportunity WHERE [condition] LIMIT 25

For name searches, use LIKE with wildcards:
  WHERE LastName LIKE '%Smith%' (case-insensitive match)
  WHERE Name LIKE '%Acme%'

For date filters:
  WHERE CloseDate >= 2026-01-01 AND CloseDate <= 2026-12-31
  WHERE CreatedDate = LAST_N_DAYS:30

### executeSoslSearch — Full-text keyword search
Use when the user wants to search across multiple object types simultaneously:
  FIND {search term} IN ALL FIELDS RETURNING Account(Id, Name, Phone), Contact(Id, FirstName, LastName, Email), Opportunity(Id, Name, StageName, Amount)

### describeObject — Field discovery
When you encounter an object (especially custom objects ending in __c) and don't know what fields are available, call describeObject first. Look at the fields array, specifically the name, label, and type properties. Then construct your SOQL query using the discovered field names.

### getNextQueryResults — Pagination
When a query response has done=false, tell the user: "There are [totalSize] total results. Showing the first batch — want to see more?" If yes, call getNextQueryResults with the identifier from nextRecordsUrl.

### Handling Unfamiliar Fields
When Salesforce returns field names the user might not understand (API names like BillingStreet, StageName, IsWon, etc.), translate them to plain English in your response. Present data in a clean, readable format — not raw JSON.

### Error Handling
- MALFORMED_QUERY: You constructed bad SOQL. Fix the syntax and retry.
- INVALID_FIELD: A field name doesn't exist on that object. Call describeObject to discover correct field names, then retry.
- INVALID_TYPE: The object name is wrong. Check spelling (API names are case-sensitive for custom objects).
- REQUEST_LIMIT_EXCEEDED: Salesforce API limits hit. Tell the user to try again in a few minutes.
- 401 Unauthorized: Token expired. Tell the user to re-authenticate by clicking the sign-in prompt.
```

### 5.3 Ambiguity Resolution

Add to the routing section:
```
If the intent is ambiguous between sources:
- "Find John Smith" --> Search Salesforce Contacts first (people = CRM)
- "Find the Smith project files" --> Search Egnyte (files = document storage)
- "What's the status of the Smith project?" --> Could be ACC (construction status) or Salesforce (deal status). Ask: "Are you looking for construction progress (RFIs, issues) or the business status (proposal stage, contract amount)?"
```

---

## 6. Testing Plan

### 6.1 Test OAuth Flow

1. **Save the GPT** with the Salesforce Action configured **[V]**
2. **Open the GPT** in a new ChatGPT conversation **[V]**
3. Type a query that would trigger the Salesforce action, e.g.: **"Look up John Smith in Salesforce"** **[V]**
4. ChatGPT should display a **"Sign in to {instance}.my.salesforce.com"** button **[V]**
5. Click it — you should be redirected to Salesforce's login page **[V]**
6. Log in with a valid Salesforce user account **[V]**
7. You should see an **"Allow Access"** screen (unless admin-preapproved) — click Allow **[V]**
8. You should be redirected back to ChatGPT, and the query should execute **[V]**

**If OAuth fails:** See Section 8 (Common Pitfalls) for debugging steps.

### 6.2 First Queries to Try

Test these in order, from simplest to most complex:

| # | Query | What It Tests |
|---|-------|---------------|
| 1 | "Show me the first 5 contacts in Salesforce" | Basic SOQL, field selection, LIMIT |
| 2 | "Find contacts with the last name Johnson" | WHERE clause with LIKE |
| 3 | "Show me all accounts in California" | Account queries, state filtering |
| 4 | "What open opportunities are there over $50,000?" | Opportunity queries, numeric/boolean filters |
| 5 | "Find everything related to Acme" | SOSL cross-object search |
| 6 | "What fields are available on the Contact object?" | Describe endpoint |
| 7 | "Show me 100 contacts" | Pagination handling (likely exceeds first batch) |
| 8 | "Look up the Riverside project files" | Multi-source routing (should go to Egnyte, not SF) |
| 9 | "What RFIs are open?" | Multi-source routing (should go to ACC, not SF) |
| 10 | "Find Tom Southam's contact info and any open deals for GCI" | Combined Salesforce query |

### 6.3 Verify Permissions

After successful OAuth, test that the token's permissions are correct:

1. **Can read Contacts?** Try query #1 above **[V]**
2. **Can read Accounts?** Try query #3 **[V]**
3. **Can read Opportunities?** Try query #4 **[V]**
4. **Custom objects accessible?** Ask "What objects are available?" and try a describe on any custom objects **[V]**
5. **Cannot write data?** This is read-only by schema design (we only defined GET endpoints). Even if the OAuth scope grants write access, the GPT has no write actions defined. **[V]**

### 6.4 Common Errors During Testing

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| "Sign in" button doesn't appear | Action not saved, or schema has errors | Re-open Action editor, check for red error indicators |
| OAuth redirect fails | Wrong callback URL in Salesforce | Verify both callback URL variants are saved in Connected App |
| "Invalid grant" after login | Connected App propagation delay, or PKCE is enabled | Wait 10 min; verify PKCE is unchecked |
| `INVALID_SESSION_ID` | Token expired or not properly passed | Re-authenticate; check that Token Exchange Method is "Default (POST)" |
| `MALFORMED_QUERY` | Bad SOQL syntax | Check the `q` parameter in the request; common issue is improper encoding |
| Empty results | Query is correct but user has restricted visibility | Check user's Salesforce profile/sharing rules |

---

## 7. Security Considerations

### 7.1 What the OAuth Token Grants Access To

The OAuth token inherits the **permissions of the authenticating user**. This means: **[V]**

- The token can access every object and field the user can see in Salesforce **[V]**
- Row-level access is governed by Salesforce's sharing model (OWD, sharing rules, role hierarchy) **[V]**
- Field-level security (FLS) applies — fields hidden from the user's profile won't appear in API responses **[V]**
- The token does NOT bypass any Salesforce security model — it operates as the user **[V]**

**Implication for GCI:** If Tom (VP) authenticates, the GPT can see everything Tom can see. If a project manager authenticates, they see data per their profile. This is the same behavior as logging into Salesforce directly.

### 7.2 Scope Selection: `full` vs `api`

| Scope | What It Grants | Risk Level |
|-------|---------------|------------|
| `full` | Access to all data accessible by the user, plus all API endpoints | Higher — full API surface area. Simplest setup. **[V]** |
| `api` | Access to REST API and Bulk API only | Lower — no Visualforce, no web UI access **[V]** |
| `api` + `refresh_token` | API access with persistent sessions | Recommended minimum for production **[V]** |

**Security best practice:** Use `api` + `refresh_token` instead of `full` for production. `full` is convenient during development but grants broader access than needed. **[SS]**

**Important caveat:** Salesforce scopes control **API surface area**, not data visibility. Whether you use `full` or `api`, the user still sees only data their profile permits. The scope determines which API endpoints are available, not which records are visible. **[V]**

**Source:** [Salesforce: OAuth Scope Parameter Values](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/mobile-sdk/guide/oauth-scope-parameter-values.html), [Nudge Security: Salesforce OAuth Scopes](https://www.nudgesecurity.com/oauth-scopes/salesforce)

### 7.3 Connected App Policy Recommendations

| Setting | Recommended | Why |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| Permitted Users | All users may self-authorize | Easiest for rollout; each user authenticates individually **[V]** |
| IP Relaxation | Relax IP restrictions | ChatGPT's IPs are dynamic/unknown **[SS]** |
| Refresh Token Policy | Valid until revoked | Prevents frequent re-authentication for GCI users **[V]** |
| Session Timeout | Use org default (typically 2 hours for access token) | Access tokens expire; refresh token handles renewal automatically **[V]** |

**For tighter security (optional):**
- Set Permitted Users to "Admin approved users are pre-authorized"
- Assign specific Profiles/Permission Sets that should have GPT access
- Set refresh token to expire after N days of inactivity

### 7.4 Data the GPT Can vs Cannot Access

**Can access (read-only, limited by schema):**
- Any standard or custom object the user has read access to
- Field values governed by field-level security
- Related records via SOQL relationship queries

**Cannot access (by schema design):**
- File attachments, binary content (schema only defines GET for text/JSON endpoints)
- Cannot create, update, or delete any records (no POST/PATCH/DELETE in schema)
- Cannot access Salesforce Setup or admin functions
- Cannot see records the authenticating user cannot see

### 7.5 Token Lifetime

| Token Type | Default Lifetime | Notes |
|------------|-----------------|-------|
| Access Token | ~2 hours (follows org session timeout) | Short-lived, auto-refreshed by ChatGPT **[V]** |
| Refresh Token | Until revoked (default policy) | Long-lived; ChatGPT uses this to get new access tokens **[V]** |

ChatGPT automatically handles refresh token exchange. When the access token expires, ChatGPT sends the refresh token to the token URL and gets a new access token. This is transparent to the user. **[V]**

**Source:** [Salesforce: OAuth Refresh Token Flow](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/mobile-sdk/guide/oauth-refresh-token-flow.html)

---

## 8. Common Pitfalls

### 8.1 PKCE Enabled (Most Common)

**Symptom:** OAuth flow fails silently, or returns an error about missing `code_verifier`
**Cause:** The "Require Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE)" checkbox is checked
**Fix:** Uncheck it in the Connected App settings. ChatGPT Actions do not implement PKCE. **[V]**

### 8.2 Connected App Propagation Delay

**Symptom:** "Invalid client" or "Invalid grant" errors immediately after creating/editing the Connected App
**Cause:** Salesforce takes 2-10 minutes to propagate Connected App changes
**Fix:** Wait at least 10 minutes after saving before testing. **[SS]**

### 8.3 Callback URL Mismatch

**Symptom:** OAuth redirects to an error page or "redirect_uri_mismatch" error
**Cause:** The callback URL in Salesforce doesn't match what ChatGPT is sending
**Fix:** Ensure BOTH callback URL variants are registered in the Connected App: **[V]**
```
https://chat.openai.com/aip/g-{GPT_ID}/oauth/callback
https://chatgpt.com/aip/g-{GPT_ID}/oauth/callback
```

### 8.4 Domain Mismatch Between Auth URLs and API Server

**Symptom:** ChatGPT shows "Auth URL, Token URL and API hostname must share a root domain"
**Cause:** Using `login.salesforce.com` for OAuth URLs while the schema server is `{instance}.my.salesforce.com`
**Fix:** Use the My Domain URL (`{instance}.my.salesforce.com`) for ALL three: Authorization URL, Token URL, and the schema's servers URL. **[V]**

### 8.5 `login.salesforce.com` vs My Domain

**Symptom:** Various auth failures or domain mismatch errors
**Explanation:** Salesforce supports both `login.salesforce.com` (generic) and `{instance}.my.salesforce.com` (org-specific) as OAuth endpoints. For ChatGPT Actions, always use the My Domain format because of the domain matching requirement. **[V]**

**For sandbox testing:** Use `{instance}--{sandbox}.sandbox.my.salesforce.com` instead. **[SS]**

### 8.6 Token Refresh Failures

**Symptom:** GPT works initially but stops working after a few hours, requiring re-authentication
**Cause:** Refresh token not being issued, or refresh token policy is too restrictive
**Fix:** **[V]**
1. Ensure `refresh_token` or `offline_access` scope is selected in the Connected App
2. Ensure the refresh token policy is "Valid until revoked" (not "Immediately expire")
3. Verify "Require Secret for Refresh Token Flow" is checked (ChatGPT sends the secret)

### 8.7 SOQL Query Too Large

**Symptom:** ChatGPT returns an error or the response is truncated/garbled
**Cause:** Query returned too many records, exceeding the 100K character response limit
**Fix:** Always include LIMIT clauses in system instructions. Default to LIMIT 25. **[V]**

### 8.8 Internal Server Error (500) from Salesforce

**Symptom:** Salesforce returns 500 errors
**Cause:** Often caused by incorrect OAuth checkbox configuration in the Connected App
**Fix:** Verify all three flow checkboxes are checked (Client Credentials, Authorization Code and Credentials, Token Exchange). **[SS]**

### 8.9 User Sees "Access Denied" or Empty Results

**Symptom:** OAuth succeeds but queries return no data
**Cause:** The authenticating user's profile lacks read access to the queried objects/fields
**Fix:** Check the user's Salesforce profile for object permissions and field-level security. The API respects all profile-level restrictions. **[V]**

---

## 9. GCI-Specific Considerations

### 9.1 Standard Salesforce Objects for Construction Companies

Construction companies typically use these standard Salesforce objects: **[SS]**

| Object | Construction Use Case | Key Fields |
|--------|----------------------|------------|
| **Account** | Clients, general contractors, subcontractors, vendors | Name, Industry, BillingAddress, Phone, Website |
| **Contact** | People at client/sub/vendor companies | FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Title, Account.Name |
| **Opportunity** | Bids, proposals, active deals | Name, StageName, Amount, CloseDate, Account.Name, Owner.Name |
| **Lead** | Prospective clients or project leads | FirstName, LastName, Company, Email, Phone, Status |
| **Task** | Follow-ups, call logs | Subject, ActivityDate, Status, Who.Name, What.Name |
| **Event** | Meetings, site visits | Subject, StartDateTime, EndDateTime, Who.Name, Location |

### 9.2 Likely Custom Objects

Construction companies often create custom objects for: **[SS]**

- **Projects** (`Project__c` or similar) — tracking active construction projects separately from Opportunities
- **Bids** (`Bid__c`) — bid tracking with dates, amounts, win/loss status
- **Change Orders** (`Change_Order__c`) — tracking scope changes and cost impacts
- **Equipment** (`Equipment__c`) — owned/leased equipment tracking
- **Subcontractor Agreements** (`Subcontract__c`) — formal sub relationships

**GCI may or may not have these.** We need to ask. The `describeObject` endpoint lets the GPT discover custom objects dynamically, but we should ask GCI upfront what custom objects they use so we can optimize the system instructions.

### 9.3 What to Ask GCI Before Implementation

1. **"What is your Salesforce My Domain URL?"** (e.g., `gci.my.salesforce.com`)
2. **"What do you primarily track in Salesforce?"** (Contacts? Accounts? Opportunities? Custom objects?)
3. **"Do you have any custom objects?"** (If yes, what are their API names?)
4. **"Who should be able to use this?"** (All users? Specific roles? Just leadership?)
5. **"Is there any sensitive data in Salesforce we should be aware of?"** (e.g., salary data, SSN, etc.)
6. **"Do you use Salesforce sandbox for testing?"** (If yes, we can test there first)

### 9.4 Most Useful Queries for Construction Companies

Based on the construction company context, the highest-value queries are: **[SS]**

1. **"What's the contact info for [person] at [company]?"** — Contact lookup
2. **"Show me all our active bids"** — Opportunity pipeline (StageName filters)
3. **"What accounts are in [city/state]?"** — Geographic account lookup
4. **"Who's our contact at [subcontractor name]?"** — Contact-Account relationship
5. **"What opportunities are closing this quarter?"** — Date-filtered pipeline
6. **"Show me everything we have on [company name]"** — SOSL cross-object search
7. **"What new leads came in this month?"** — Lead tracking with date filters

---

## 10. Critical Timing Issue: Spring '26 Changes

### 10.1 Connected App Creation Restriction

Starting with the Salesforce **Spring '26 release** (rolling out January 16 - February 20, 2026), **new Connected App creation is disabled by default**. Salesforce is phasing out Connected Apps in favor of External Client Apps (ECAs). **[V]**

**Impact on GCI:**
- If GCI's org has already received the Spring '26 update, they may not be able to create a new Connected App through the UI
- They would need to either: (a) contact Salesforce Support to temporarily re-enable Connected App creation, or (b) use the new External Client App flow instead
- **Existing Connected Apps continue to work** — this only affects creating new ones **[V]**

### 10.2 Recommended Approach

1. **First, try creating a Connected App** via Setup > App Manager > New Connected App
2. If the option is grayed out or unavailable, GCI's org has received the Spring '26 update
3. In that case, use **External Client Apps** instead — the OAuth setup is similar but the navigation path is different

### 10.3 External Client App Alternative (if Connected App creation is blocked)

**Navigation:** Setup > search for **"External Client App Manager"** > New External Client App **[SS]**

The OAuth configuration fields are largely the same (consumer key, consumer secret, callback URL, scopes). The key differences: **[SS]**

- ECAs are managed in a separate UI (External Client App Manager, not App Manager)
- ECAs support the same OAuth flows as Connected Apps
- ECAs are the forward-looking standard and will eventually replace Connected Apps entirely

**Verification status:** The ECA OAuth flow for ChatGPT Actions is **UNVERIFIED** — the OpenAI Cookbook example uses Connected Apps. The flows should be compatible since ECAs support the same OAuth 2.0 grants, but this needs to be tested.

**Source:** [Salesforce Ben: External Client Apps vs Connected Apps](https://www.salesforceben.com/external-client-vs-connected-apps-comparing-salesforces-next-gen-integration/), [Salesforce: Phasing Out Connected Apps (Spring '26)](https://www.revenueopsllc.com/salesforce-is-phasing-out-connected-apps-heres-what-the-spring-26-change-really-means/)

## Appendix A: Quick Reference Card

### GCI Admin Needs to Provide
1. Salesforce My Domain URL (e.g., `gci.my.salesforce.com`)
2. Consumer Key (from Connected App)
3. Consumer Secret (from Connected App)
4. List of objects/fields they want searchable
5. Whether they have custom objects (and their API names)

### ChatGPT Action Config (Quick Fill)
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Auth Type | OAuth |
| Client ID | `{Consumer Key}` |
| Client Secret | `{Consumer Secret}` |
| Auth URL | `https://{instance}.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize` |
| Token URL | `https://{instance}.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token` |
| Scope | `full` |
| Token Method | Default (POST) |

### Key Constraints
| Constraint | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| API Response limit | 100K characters |
| API Timeout | 45 seconds |
| Content type | Text/JSON only (no binary) |
| PKCE | NOT supported by ChatGPT |
| Default SOQL batch size | 2000 records |
| Recommended LIMIT | 25-50 records per query |
| Connected App propagation | 2-10 minutes after save |
| Access token lifetime | ~2 hours (auto-refreshed) |
| Refresh token lifetime | Until revoked (default) |

| 3 | Update callback URL in Salesforce | 10 min |
| 4 | Update system instructions for multi-source routing | 1-2 hours |
| 5 | Test OAuth flow + basic queries | 30 min |
